rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LUIS CARRILLO TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Respondent Mark Nelson To Compel Obedience To Subpoena By Third-Party Witness, Paul Camera, And For Imposition On Paul Camera Of Monetary Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 2030.030
rulings.Rulings: The Respondent's motion to compel subpoenaed business records is GRANTED in part. The subpoenaed records are discoverable under CCP section 2017.010. The subpoena was properly served, and Camera, the subpoenaed party, has not filed a motion to quash, motion for protective order, or other proper objection to the subpoena. Camera must produce to Respondent responsive documents within his custody or control that he has not already produced to Respondent, including documents on his personal computer. The Court rejects Camera's unilateral narrowing of the information requested given the liberal scope of discoverable material. The Court is not convinced that the burden of making a full production is unreasonable under the circumstances. Given that Camera had access to the Savvy server now controlled by a third party while this subpoena was pending but did not use that access to provide all required responsive documents to Respondent, Camera must also inform that third party that he does not object to production of responsive documents to Respondent, and Camera must cooperate with the third party to the extent necessary for the third party to access Camera's documents on the server (including providing any needed password to the third party or unlocking access to the information by inputting the password himself). To the extent Camera contends that responsive documents would include privileged financial information of other third parties (a fact of which he has not convinced the Court on these papers), this order is without prejudice to the parties negotiating a protective order allowing redaction or limiting the use of any such information. The Respondent's request for sanctions is GRANTED in part in the amount of $1,400. The subpoenaed party has misused the discovery process, inter alia, by failing to comply fully with the subpoena, making evasive responses to the subpoena, and prematurely cutting off the meet and confer process. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling must so advise all other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:30 p.m. on Wednesday, April 15, 2015. Counsel for the moving party is directed to submit a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE ROBIN WILLIAMS 1996 IRREVOCABLE TR. DTD. 8/7/96 TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To File Records Under Seal In Connection With Petition For Appointment Of Co-Trustee And Modification Of Trust; Memorandum In Support Thereof
rulings.Rulings: The Co-Trustees' Motion to File Records Under Seal is DENIED. The Co-Trustees have failed to show that their interest in keeping the documents private overrules the public's interest in open access to court records. (CRC, Rule 2.550(d); NBC Subsidiary (KNBC-TV), Inc. v. Superior Court (Locke) (1999) 20 Cal.4th 1178, 1216-1218.) Further, the Co-Trustees have not shown that the proposed sealing is the least restrictive means to achieve their interest in keeping these documents confidential. Any party choosing to contest the tentative shall notify the Court and all interested parties no later than 4/15/2015, at 4:00 p.m. The Co-Trustees shall submit a proposed order that reflects the tentative ruling.
